chunks.text: Distress Tolerance Worksheet 14 (p. 2 of 3) Use skills (things to do to avoid urges, interpersonal effectiveness, distress tolerance, emotion ВүВү regulation, mindfulness). The most useful skills for me include: Find alternative ways to rebel. These include: ВүВү Publicly announce IвҖҷve embraced abstinence and effective behavior. ВүВү Plan for Harm Reduction If I have a slip, I donвҖҷt want the slip to turn into a slide. To avoid a slide, I must have plans to regain my balance and get back to abstinence and effectiveness. Call my therapist, sponsor, or mentor for skills coaching. His or her number is: ВүВү Get in contact with other effective people who can help (e.g., friends or family, people from ВүВү group). These people include (with contact information): Get rid of the temptations (e.g., drugs, comfort food); surround myself with cues for effective ВүВү behaviors (e.g., workout clothes, fruit). Review skills and handouts from DBT. The most helpful skills/handouts for me are: ВүВү Opposite action (Emotion Regulation Handout 10) can be rehearsed to fight guilt and shame. If ВүВү no other option works, go to an anonymous meeting of any sort and publicly report your lapse. Building mastery and coping ahead for emotional situations (Emotion Regulation Handout 19), ВүВү and checking the facts (Emotion Regulation Handout 8), can be used to fight feelings of being out of control. (continued on next page)

chunks.heading: Distress Tolerance Worksheet 14 (p. 3 of 3)
chunks.preview: Distress Tolerance Worksheet 14 (p. 3 of 3) Interpersonal skills (Interpersonal Effectiveness Handouts 5вҖ“7), such as asking for help from ВүВү family, friends, sponsors, ministers, or counselors, can also be helpful. If you are isolated, help can often be found via online support groups. These people...
chunks.text: Distress Tolerance Worksheet 14 (p. 3 of 3) Interpersonal skills (Interpersonal Effectiveness Handouts 5вҖ“7), such as asking for help from ВүВү family, friends, sponsors, ministers, or counselors, can also be helpful. If you are isolated, help can often be found via online support groups. These people or groups include: Conduct a chain analysis to analyze what prompted the lapse (General Handouts 7, 7a). ВүВү Problem-solve right away to find a way to вҖңget back on the wagonвҖқ and repair any damage you ВүВү have done (Emotion Regulation Handout 12). Distract yourself, self-Вӯsoothe, and improve the moment. ВүВү Cheerlead myself (e.g., вҖңOne slip is not a disaster,вҖқ вҖңDonвҖҷt give up,вҖқ вҖңDonвҖҷt get willful,вҖқ вҖңI can still ВүВү climb back on the wagon.вҖқ) My cheerleading statements will include: Do pros and cons of stopping again now. ВүВү Stay away from extreme thinking. Always look for the middle ground. DonвҖҷt let one slip turn into ВүВү a disaster. (Check each extreme thought I am giving up and the middle ground I am accepting.) Extreme thinking: Middle ground: I have not quit yet; therefore I am ВүВү Relapsing once does not doom me to ВүВү doomed and might as well give up. never stopping. Now that IвҖҷve relapsed, I might as well ВүВү I relapsed, but that does not mean ВүВү keep going. I have to stay relapsed. I can be effective and get up now. I missed an appointment, so IвҖҷm done ВүВү I missed an appointment, but I can get ВүВү with treatment. in to see my therapist ASAP. Other: ВүВү Other: ВүВү Other: ВүВү Other: ВүВү Recommit to 100% total abstinence. ВүВү

During the week, write down the clear mind behavior you did to replace clean mind. Clean Mind Behaviors Clear Mind Behaviors as Replacements ВүВү1. Engaging in apparently irrelevant behaviors that in the past inevitably led to problem behavior. ВүВү2. Thinking, вҖңIвҖҷve learned my lesson.вҖқ ВүВү3. Believing, вҖңI can control my addiction.вҖқ ВүВү4. Thinking, вҖңI donвҖҷt really have an addiction.вҖқ ВүВү5. Stopping or cutting back medication that helps with addiction. ВүВү6. Being in addiction environments. ВүВү7. Seeing friends who are still addicted. ВүВү8. Living with people who are addicted. ВүВү9. Keeping addiction paraphernalia. 10. Carrying around extra money. ВүВү 11. Being irresponsible with bills. ВүВү 12. Dressing like an addict. ВүВү 13. Not going to meetings. ВүВү 14. Isolating. ВүВү 15. Believing, вҖңI can do this alone.вҖқ ВүВү 16. Ignoring problems fueling addiction. ВүВү 17. Acting as if I only need willpower. ВүВү 18. Thinking, вҖңI donвҖҷt need to say ВүВү anything about my addiction.вҖқ 19. Thinking, вҖңI canвҖҷt stand this!вҖқ ВүВү 20. Other: ВүВү 21. Other: ВүВү From DBT Skills Training Handouts and Worksheets, Second Edition, by Marsha M. Linehan. Copyright 2015 by Marsha M. Linehan.
